The UNDECIDED route (RT-1) fires when no mechanism PASS exists and the H0-NO pattern is blocked by an INCONCLUSIVE or repeated INSTRUMENT-INVALID after its replication buy: publish with the pre-computed decidability bands + a what-budget/n-would-decide statement. **Replication-buy cap (pre-declared):** at most one replication buy per experiment/gap, at most two programme-wide; "narrow-and-continue" is never invocable twice for the same missing evidence (GR-9) | **GATE-H** over AUTO-computed route | f1, g2–g9, f2, e9, f4, g1, e8r (e8d if run), f3, f6, m0b closes; a-extrap-2.close; a-h0.readout | signed GNG-2 record; routes Tier 4/5; PIVOT/KILL/UNDECIDED ⇒ write-up phase pulls forward (§5) |

docs/research-plan/03-operational-dag.md-435-| # | Rule | Enforcement |
docs/research-plan/03-operational-dag.md-436-|---|---|---|
docs/research-plan/03-operational-dag.md:437:| **GR-1** | **Per-tier hard budget caps** (canonical table, RT-8 reconciliation — identical wherever a cap table appears, incl. P2 G-11 and P6 §4; maintainer confirms at I-BUDGET): Tier 0 ≤ $20 (API only) · Tier 1 ≤ $80 · Tier 2 ≤ $400 · Tier 3 ≤ $400 · cumulative Tiers 0–3 ≤ $900 · Tier 4 (F5) ≤ $900 · Tier 5 = maintainer-approved envelope ($2–10k). Worst-case Tiers 0–3 ≈ $760 < $900. **Freeze-time tier-sum lint:** Σ(frozen worst-cases in a tier) ≤ tier cap — `prereg-freeze` refuses a freeze that would break the sum, so a fully-utilised tier can never BUDGET-HALT mid-campaign by arithmetic. | Pre-launch: harness refuses any RunSpec whose worst_case_usd exceeds remaining tier headroom (`ERR_BUDGET`). In-flight: hourly cost monitor polls Modal spend + ledger; breach ⇒ **kill switch**: `modal app stop` on every live app in the tier + `poc/gpu/terminate.sh` for any AWS boxes + BUDGET-HALT status event in the log (P2 G-11) + bead filed + maintainer notified. Every container additionally carries its sized hard timeout (a wedged run self-caps). Resumption only via a dated ops amendment raising the cap. |
docs/research-plan/03-operational-dag.md-438-| **GR-2** | **Hash-pin enforcement.** No run without in-container staged-manifest + encoder + corpus + prereg-hash assertions; all fail closed with named `ERR_*` codes. Any pin change = new pre-registration, reported as such. | §3.3 mechanisms; P2 G-1/G-14: `log-append` embeds and verifies the prereg hash, demotes drifted-pin runs to exploratory; verdict-gen step 3 excludes them visibly. |
docs/research-plan/03-operational-dag.md:439:| **GR-3** | **≤5 concurrent agents, waves, no nested spawning.** Coordinators never spawn children-of-children; runner waves sized ≤5 (the fleet has been rate-limit-killed twice at higher concurrency). | Wave schedule in §5; coordinator launch discipline; beads wave labels; a runner that needs parallelism uses Modal `starmap` (containers, not agents). |
docs/research-plan/03-operational-dag.md-440-| **GR-4** | **Fail-closed gates.** A gate that cannot evaluate (missing verdict object, unreadable ledger, absent human record) is CLOSED. No silent fallbacks anywhere in harness code (house rule). | AUTO-GATE scripts return CLOSED on any exception; scheduler treats CLOSED = blocked; the shared expression evaluator (P2 §3.1 grammar) is the only gate semantics. |
docs/research-plan/03-operational-dag.md-441-| **GR-5** | **Negative-result honesty.** Every completed run appends to the chained log regardless of outcome; nulls require TOST to be called NULL (else INCONCLUSIVE); negative verdicts are rendered by the same template at the same prominence and the paper's results table enumerates every CLOSED experiment mechanically. | P2 G-5 + P2 §3.3 template; report-gen refuses to render a PASS without its verbatim kill-criterion text alongside (P1 §6 anti-overselling guard, mechanised); paper.lint re-checks at manuscript level (GR-15). |
docs/research-plan/03-operational-dag.md:442:| **GR-6** | **Run-vs-audit separation.** The agent identity that ran or built any part of an experiment never audits it; a computed PASS is PASS-PENDING-AUDIT until a CONFIRMED audit record by a distinct identity lands; audits re-derive the verdict from pinned artifacts, adversarially (leakage, tuning asymmetry, endpoint drift, arm-favouring bugs — P4 checklist). Positives get full audits; kills/nulls get conformance audits. The paper gets the same treatment (paper.review). Externally the property is reported as **role-separated re-derivation**, never "independent audit" (P2 G-6 naming discipline, RT-9); "independent" is reserved for maintainer-level audits and genuinely external replications. | P2 G-6 schema rule (`ERR_P2_SELF_AUDIT`: auditor ≠ every runner in the eligible log); agent ids in RunSpec + audit record; backup Fable account for hard identity separation (O-5). |
docs/research-plan/03-operational-dag.md-443-| **GR-7** | **Single-look / no endpoint drift.** Analysis scripts hashed at X.reg; the primary endpoint is computed once by the pinned script at X.readout (which writes the `unblind` line; note the **design-amendment cutoff is earlier** — the first `phase:"final"` run record, P2 §1.4/G-3, RT-5); any additional analysis is `phase:"exploratory"` (quarantined, uncitable) and can never flip a verdict. | P2 G-3/G-4/G-13: frozen analysis_script sha, amendment validity vs the first-final-run line, exploration quarantine + citation scanner. |
docs/research-plan/03-operational-dag.md-444-| **GR-8** | **No auto-commit of results from containers; no auto-push of claims.** Coordinator reviews and commits; external communication of any result is GATE-H (paper.submit / xb.deliver / ad-hoc pitches all included). | Existing transport behaviour; §2 gate class 7; GR-9/GR-15 lints upstream of any exposure. |
docs/research-plan/03-operational-dag.md-445-| **GR-9** | **Claim-discipline lints** (mechanised in report-gen + `registry-check --citations`): every quoted number carries rung + M0b coverage + comparison discipline — plus the **NICHE-SCOPE banner** whenever m0b.gate fired below the GNG-0 threshold (RT-7); scale adjectives require the "slope" license (≥3 rungs; 2 = sign only; 1 = nothing) computed into the verdict object; no strong-A1 restatement unless HS13 passed; **replication-buy cap** (RT-1): at most one replication buy per experiment/gap, at most two programme-wide, and "narrow-and-continue" never invocable twice for the same missing evidence; m0b.close ≺ any external quotation. | P2 G-7/G-12 + the citations scanner over `docs/` and `reports/`; a-extrap-* nodes are the only source of extrapolation statements. |

item.text:
Not sound to act on as written. The core adjudication is directionally right, but the remediation spec conflicts with current programme law and the audit/registry state is incompletely described.

## Three most important concerns

1. **The proposed generator fix rewrites programme law while presenting it as an implementation repair.**  
   The document says three rungs cap language at `sign-only` and attributes that to §2.3 ([adjudication:73](/home/ec2-user/css/kernel/kernel-of-truth/docs/next/a5-llm-refute-adjudication.md:73)). That is false. The governing text says ≥3 comparable rungs license a scale-trend fit ([08-stats:326](/home/ec2-user/css/kernel/kernel-of-truth/docs/research-plan/08-stats-and-extrapolation.md:326)), and G-12 explicitly maps ≥3 rungs to `slope` ([02-data:664](/home/ec2-user/css/kernel/kernel-of-truth/docs/research-plan/02-data-and-reporting.md:664)). Section 2.3 says a three-rung prediction interval is weak/nearly vacuous, not prohibited.

   The a5-llm defect is nevertheless real because R0 is not a model-scale rung, its frozen endpoint expressly says “never a slope law,” and its scale-trend instrument failed. The minimal fix should enforce a machine-readable frozen per-record ceiling, identify comparable model-scale rungs, and require the registered trend machinery to have produced a valid result. A global “slope requires ≥4” rule is a maintainer-level policy change, not a routine generator bug fix.

2. **The audit history and update mechanics are materially incomplete and internally contradictory.**  
   There are three audits, not merely “two audits … refusing the object”:

   - 2026-07-09 REJECT on the pin.
   - 2026-07-10 CONFIRM with correction/1 in scope.
   - 2026-07-11 REFUTE on pin handling plus slope licensing.

   The audit ledger currently says a5-llm is CONFIRMED, while the new REFUTE has not been reconciled into formal registry audit state. The document must say that the later REFUTE invalidates the prior confirmation’s completeness, then require ledger, assessment, synthesis, audit record, and audit-ref reconciliation.

   It also says the verdict is append-never-edit ([adjudication:83](/home/ec2-user/css/kernel/kernel-of-truth/docs/next/a5-llm-refute-adjudication.md:83)) but later proposes to “flip the verdict’s audit state” ([adjudication:118](/home/ec2-user/css/kernel/kernel-of-truth/docs/next/a5-llm-refute-adjudication.md:118)). Programme documentation says a CONFIRMED audit is followed by an S7 rerun that upgrades the verdict. The maintainer needs one explicit ruling on whether verdict regeneration is lawful and how correction/1’s old verdict hash remains traceable.

3. **The efficiency-thesis language remains too broad.**  
   The numerical comparison survives, but “verifier-offload” and “efficiency-thesis evidence … intact” ([adjudication:107](/home/ec2-user/css/kernel/kernel-of-truth/docs/next/a5-llm-refute-adjudication.md:107)) overstate what a5-llm measured. It is a marginal-inference-cost substrate comparison on formal, self-authored queries against the engine’s own typed store. It has no NL boundary, conventional typed-store/checker control, all-in authoring/indexing cost, deployable RAG claim, or evidence of kernel usefulness to a model. Call it narrow evidence for cheap deterministic execution on the measured slice—not verifier-offload evidence generally.

## Assessment against the requested questions

1. **Pin mismatch:** Correct and well evidenced. The frozen, executed, corpus-mirror, and current hashes reproduce; all nine runs used `b622…`; `engine_matches_a5=1` establishes per-query outcome identity against the `d206…` parent run. Benign for these endpoints, substantive as a record-integrity defect. No rerun or second pin erratum is warranted.

2. **Seventeen-verdict sweep:** Correct as an over-licensing sweep.

   - `a5-llm`: defective `slope`.
   - `f2`: defective/at-risk `slope`; its frozen envelope says direction-only.
   - `f2b-replicate`: correctly `sign-only`.
   - Remaining 14: `none`, hence no over-license.

   Qualify “cleared”: the 14 were cleared against this over-licensing failure, not comprehensively re-derived for possible conservative under-licensing.

3. **Remediation set and order:** The broad components are right, but the specification and ordering need revision:

   1. Maintainer decides whether this is per-record enforcement or a global ≥4-rung law change.
   2. Specify the machine-readable license source and verdict-regeneration policy.
   3. Issue correction/2 and implement/test both generators plus report text.
   4. Reconcile the stale CONFIRM ledger/assessment/audit history.
   5. Run an independent Gate-A audit with both corrections and the corrected licensing rule in scope.
   6. Record audit-ref and regenerate/upgrade the verdict only under the settled policy.

   The f2 erratum and freeze-time pin lint are appropriate follow-ups but do not block a5-llm re-audit.

4. **Net standing:** `PASS-PENDING-AUDIT` is numerically supportable and likely confirmable, but currently not confirmed after the later REFUTE. “Metadata field” understates the slope defect: it is claim-governance metadata, not harmless display metadata. Also, excluding truncated rows is not the “harshest” pro-LLM sensitivity; crediting every truncated item as correct is harsher and yields a 0.462 effect bound. The document’s 0.577 statement is still accurate as complete-case sensitivity.

5. **Authority scoping:** Over-gated overall. GATE-A is role-separated but automatable; routine code/test work and audit scheduling are coordinator-executable under the standing DAG. Fable can author an interpretive correction under coordinator delegation, following precedent. Maintainer involvement is genuinely required if changing the global three-rung licensing law or resolving the verdict immutability/regeneration ambiguity—not necessarily for every listed action.

## Factual errors to correct

- §2.3 does not cap three rungs at sign-only.
- The current normative G-12 rule explicitly maps ≥3 rungs to `slope`.
- There have been three audits, including an intervening CONFIRM.
- The ledger currently represents a5-llm as CONFIRMED, not pending.
- “Harshest row-handling reading” incorrectly describes the 0.577 complete-case sensitivity.
- “Append-never-edit verdict” conflicts with the documented S7 post-audit upgrade path and the document’s own proposed audit-state flip.

**Recommendation:** commit only after these corrections. The pin adjudication, slope diagnosis, and narrow conclusion that the numerical PASS remains confirmable are sound. Do not authorize the proposed generator implementation verbatim; settle the licensing law and verdict-update semantics first.
